system_description.overview: Lumari is an AI-first platform that helps teams leverage AI to improve operational capabilities. It provides AI-powered workflows and tooling for business operations, deployed via Vercel with GCP backend services.

products.Lumari Platform: AI-first platform for teams to automate and improve operational workflows using AI capabilities

network_architecture.network_segmentation: Segmented VPCs with ACLs on GCP; separate network boundaries for Vercel edge, Supabase, and GCP backend services
network_architecture.firewalls: VPC firewall rules and ACLs on GCP
network_architecture.ids_ips: GCP-native threat detection (equivalent to GuardDuty on GCP)
network_architecture.vpn: VPN bastion host for administrative access to production
network_architecture.tls: TLS enforced for all data in transit
network_architecture.topology: Services: Backend API → Supabase (DB) + Sentry (error tracking) + Gemini (AI); AI Backend Service; Worker Service | Deployment: Vercel + GitHub | Management: Google Workspace + Linear

application_architecture.pattern: Microservices (separate Backend API, AI Backend, Worker Service)
application_architecture.environments: Separate development, staging, and production environments

authentication_access_control.mfa: MFA enforced for all production system access
authentication_access_control.password_policy: Minimum length and complexity enforced; unique user IDs required

encryption.at_rest: Cloud provider KMS (GCP KMS / Supabase encryption)

ci_cd_devops.branch_protection: Branch protection enforced; pull requests required for merges to main
ci_cd_devops.patch_management: Automated patch management; regular OS and dependency updates

third_party_services.purpose: Primary cloud infrastructure (compute, networking, VPC, storage)
Application hosting, CI/CD, edge network, logging
PostgreSQL database, authentication, row-level security, automated backups
Error tracking and application performance monitoring

security_tools.antivirus: Installed on all endpoints; continuous scanning with weekly definition updates

excluded_criteria: CC6.4 — Physical access controls managed by GCP and Vercel as subservice organizations
A1.2 — Environmental controls managed by building management and cloud providers
notable_controls: All 5 trust service criteria in scope (Security, Availability, Processing Integrity, Confidentiality, Privacy) — unusually comprehensive for a Type 1
Row-level security enforced at Supabase database layer
Sentry integration provides proactive error monitoring and incident detection
Google Gemini as critical AI dependency creates a novel third-party AI risk

infrastructure.rationale: GCP multi-AZ with Vercel edge hosting is solid. No IaC mentioned, no multi-region, and split primary hosting between GCP and Vercel adds complexity. Five trust criteria shows maturity of compliance intent.

application_architecture.rationale: Three distinct services (Backend API, AI Backend, Worker) indicate a microservices approach. Branch protection and code review enforced. No typed language explicitly confirmed, no test framework mentioned.

data_layer.rationale: Single database (Supabase PostgreSQL) with daily backups, multi-AZ, per-tenant RLS. No caching, no data warehouse. Simpler than CentralAxis's dual-DB approach.

security_posture.rationale: IDS (GCP-native), Sentry APM, monthly vuln scanning, annual pen test, endpoint AV, MFA enforced, quarterly access reviews. No WAF explicitly mentioned. 5-criteria SOC 2 shows security breadth.

devops_maturity.rationale: GitHub + Vercel CI/CD with branch protection and code review. No IaC. Separate environments. Linear for project management shows process maturity.

bcdr_readiness.rationale: Policy documented, annually reviewed and tested, multi-AZ, daily backups. No RTO/RPO disclosed. No multi-region failover.

vendor_diversity.rationale: 8 named vendors across infrastructure, AI, monitoring, and collaboration. GCP, Vercel, Supabase, Gemini, GitHub, Sentry, Google Workspace, Linear. Some Google concentration (GCP + Gemini + Workspace) but overall good diversity.

overall.rationale: Strong compliance posture for a SOC 2 Type 1 with all 5 trust criteria. Well-architected microservices with good third-party tooling. Key concerns: no IaC, no WAF, RTO/RPO undisclosed, heavy Google dependency (GCP + Gemini + Workspace), and AI supply chain risk via Gemini.

red_flags: No IaC tooling mentioned — infrastructure changes appear to be manual
RTO and RPO not disclosed despite BCDR policy existing
Critical dependency on Google Gemini for AI capabilities — AI supply chain risk with limited alternatives disclosed
No WAF mentioned despite all 5 trust criteria in scope
yellow_flags: Google concentration risk: GCP (infra) + Gemini (AI) + Google Workspace (collaboration) — three critical dependencies on single ecosystem
No caching layer or data warehouse
Vercel and GCP as dual primary infrastructure providers adds operational complexity
AI product with Gemini dependency means product functionality is tightly coupled to a third-party AI vendor's availability and pricing
green_flags: All 5 SOC 2 trust criteria in scope (Security, Availability, Processing Integrity, Confidentiality, Privacy) — unusually comprehensive for Type 1
Three-service microservices architecture (Backend API, AI Backend, Worker) shows thoughtful separation of concerns
Sentry for error tracking provides proactive monitoring beyond basic logging
Monthly automated vulnerability scanning with annual external pen test
MFA enforced for all production access
Quarterly access reviews with 1 business day revocation SLA
Zero exceptions in SOC 2 Type 1 report
Linear for project management shows process maturity beyond typical early-stage startup
key_observations: Having all 5 trust service criteria in a Type 1 report is aggressive and signals strong compliance intent — this may be driven by enterprise customer requirements
The three-service architecture (Backend API, AI Backend, Worker) explicitly separating AI processing is architecturally mature for an early-stage AI company
Google concentration across infrastructure (GCP), AI (Gemini), and collaboration (Workspace) is a notable single-ecosystem dependency that deserves diligence on contingency plans
Sentry integration at this stage suggests the team has a culture of observability and proactive incident management
The combination of Supabase for auth + database and Vercel for deployment is a well-trodden pattern for AI startups; it trades vendor concentration for speed of development
